name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash offers payday loans up to $425, title loans up to $4,000, and lines of credit in Memphis and surrounding areas with same-day or next-day funding.
description_long:
Speedy Cash operates as a short-term lending provider with multiple physical locations across Memphis, Tennessee and nearby areas. The company has established itself in the emergency cash lending market, focusing on consumers who need immediate access to funds for unexpected expenses or cash flow gaps.

The company offers three primary lending products: payday loans (up to $425 with repayment due on the next payday), lines of credit (up to $4,000 with flexible draw-down and repayment over time), and title loans (up to $4,000 secured by vehicle collateral). Additionally, Speedy Cash provides money services including check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and Green Dot Visa debit cards at their retail locations. Applications can be initiated online or completed in-store with assistance from their staff.

Speecy Cash distinguishes itself through its brick-and-mortar retail presence, offering in-person service where customers can ask questions throughout the application and funding process. The company emphasizes transparent pricing, stating customers "will know exactly how much it costs to borrow the funds prior to signing your loan agreement." They operate extended hours (typically 9am-7pm weekdays, reduced weekend hours) and maintain a local customer service team at their Memphis location.

However, as a short-term lender, Speedy Cash operates in a high-cost lending category. While the website emphasizes transparency and customer service, the actual APR and total cost of borrowing are not disclosed on this page. Payday loans and title loans are inherently expensive products with annual percentage rates often exceeding 300-400%. These loans are designed for temporary cash needs, not ongoing debt solutions, and borrowers should understand the full cost structure before committing.
pros: Multiple loan products available: payday loans ($425), lines of credit ($4,000), and title loans ($4,000) for different needs
In-store application support with staff available to answer questions throughout the process
Transparent pricing policy—borrowers know exact costs before signing loan documents
Extended business hours at Memphis locations (9am-7pm weekdays) for accessibility
Additional money services on-site: check cashing, wire transfers, money orders, and Green Dot debit cards
Multiple Memphis-area locations (9 stores listed) providing convenient access
Online application option available alongside in-store service
cons: APR and total cost of borrowing not disclosed on website—typical payday/title loan rates often exceed 300-400% APR
Payday loans capped at $425 may be insufficient for larger emergencies, forcing borrowers to use higher-cost title loans
Title loans require vehicle collateral and clear title (with state-specific restrictions), creating repossession risk if borrower cannot repay
Lines of credit up to $4,000 still represent high-cost debt relative to traditional personal loans or credit unions
No indication of credit-building features or pathways to traditional lending despite being in emergency-cash category
best_for: Employed consumers with immediate cash needs ($100-$425) due to unexpected expenses or timing gaps before payday
Vehicle owners who need larger emergency loans ($1,000-$4,000) and can safely collateralize a car title
Borrowers in Memphis/surrounding areas seeking in-person application assistance and verification before committing to terms
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for employed Memphis-area residents facing immediate, short-term cash needs who prefer in-person service and transparency over online-only lenders. The primary caveat is that all products are high-cost debt solutions (payday and title loans typically carry 300%+ APR); borrowers must understand they are paying premium rates for speed and accessibility and should exhaust lower-cost alternatives (credit unions, employer advances, family loans) before applying.
